the sidereal equivalent comes to 2 h.-44 m.-l 1 s. As the birth
(i.e. L. M. T.) is before noon, this figure, viz. 2 h.-44 m.-ll s.,
should be subtracted from the S. T. at noon. We can get this
S. T. at noon either from the Ephemeris for the year or from
the "Tables of Ascendants". From Table I in the latter we
get 1! h.-7 m.-27 s. against September 8th. From Table II
we get-j-0 m.-46 s. for 1957. So we have to add 46 seconds to
II h.-7 m.-27 s. That comes to 11 h.-8 m.-I3 s. In the Ephe-
meris it is given as 11 h. -8 m.-14 s. This is not all. We have to
make the correction to the Indian S. T. at noon. We find this
to be plus 6 seconds for Bombay. So the final S. T. at noon
becomes II h.-8 m.-20 s. To get the S. T. for the moment
of birth you will now deduct the figure 2 h.-44 m.-l 1 s. (the
sidereal equivalent) from the S. T. at noon viz. 11 h.-8 m.-
20 s. This will give you the required S. T., 8 h.-24 m.9 s. On
page 29 of the Tables you will get the ascendants for Bombay in
